type: DEFER
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
spec: Add a new module-level variable named hash_sha256 in outlines/types/__init__.py, placed near the other regex-based types (e.g. after the isbn definition). Definition: hash_sha256 = Regex(r'[a-fA-F0-9]{64}'). Optionally precede it with a one-line comment like '# Matches a SHA-256 hash: 64 hexadecimal characters' for consistency with nearby types. No other changes needed (Regex class already provides matches(), validate(), __get_pydantic_core_schema__, __get_pydantic_json_schema__, and DSL operations like optional()/at_least()).
note: hash_sha256 type, deferring to your ownership of the file

type: DEFER
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
spec: Add a new module-level variable named 'ssn' of type Regex (from outlines.types.dsl), placed immediately after the existing 'isbn' Regex definition (currently the last statement in the file, around line 68-70). Definition: ssn = Regex(r'\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}'). This matches the standard US Social Security Number format XXX-XX-XXXX (3 digits, hyphen, 2 digits, hyphen, 4 digits), e.g. '123-45-6789'. Please add a short comment above it, e.g. '# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.' No other changes needed (no new imports required, Regex is already imported from outlines.types.dsl).
note: Deferring outlines/types/__init__.py to you; go ahead and own it, add hash_sha256 and this ssn spec together.
